scenario: Ridge Healthcare schedules clinical follow-ups in Camunda 8.8 across 3000 tasks per day. A modeller wants to check whether a variable named visitDuration that holds a value such as duration("P3M") is a months-style duration on the sequence flow condition. The team needs the documented type name from the instance of section on the FEEL boolean expressions page.
question: Which instance of type name on the FEEL boolean expressions page matches duration("P3M") as a months-style duration?

options.text: duration("P3M") instance of duration returns true; the docs list duration as a top-level type name on the instance of section that covers both days and time durations and years and months durations on a single FEEL keyword
duration("P3M") instance of years and months duration returns true; the docs list years and months duration as one of the available type names for instance of on the FEEL boolean expressions page
duration("P3M") instance of period returns true; the docs list period as the type name for months-style durations on the instance of section of the FEEL boolean expressions page

duration("P3M") instance of Any returns false; the docs state that Any matches only non-null primitive values such as number, string, and boolean, and duration values are excluded from the Any type at evaluation time

a.text: Incorrect. The instance of section lists days and time duration and years and months duration as separate type names rather than a single duration type, so duration("P3M") instance of duration is not the documented form.

b.text: Correct. The Instance of section on the FEEL boolean expressions page lists years and months duration as one of the available type names and shows duration("P3M") instance of years and months duration returning true for a months-style duration value.

c.text: Incorrect. The instance of section does not list period as a type name; the documented type names for durations are days and time duration and years and months duration.

d.text: Incorrect. The instance of section states that Any matches any value that is not null, so a duration value is matched by Any rather than excluded; the section uses Any specifically to check that a value is not null.

explanation: The Instance of section on the FEEL boolean expressions page lists the available type names, including days and time duration and years and months duration, and shows duration("P3M") instance of years and months duration returning true. A months-style duration matches the years and months duration type name.
